note:
太阳能是清洁的，但它通常会占用大片土地。在印度，另一种选择是将该国的运河变成闪闪发光的太阳能电池板小径。
当刺眼的正午阳光照射在印度西部古吉拉特邦一个尘土飞扬的小村庄时，钢铁支撑结构上一排排蓝色的太阳能电池板蜿蜒地伸向地平线。这些面板覆盖在灌溉渠的顶部，像彩虹般的镜子一样闪闪发光。这个有40户人家的小村庄，有茅草墙和铁皮屋顶，还有笨重的流浪牛，是直到最近还没有电的印度农村社区之一。但现在每家每户都有一盏灯照亮，这样孩子们就可以在晚上学习，农民们可以在日落后很久才能给奶牛挤奶。
印度传统上依赖燃煤发电厂，2018-19年，该国72%的电力来自燃煤发电厂。印度阳光充足-一年中约有300个晴天-加上大量渴求能源的人口，使其成为太阳能的理想地点。截至2020年第一季度末，中国太阳能装机容量达到36.6GW，目标是到2022年增长到100GW。
但建造太阳能发电场的主要挑战之一是找到合适的地点进行这项工作。在印度，土地相对昂贵，而且往往有多个所有者，因此购买土地涉及很多手续。印度的高人口密度也给土地带来了压力，平均每平方公里有464人。屋顶太阳能电池板是一种解决方案，但建筑物顶部的阳光空间也是有限的。
在古吉拉特邦，答案一直是用太阳能电池板覆盖运河，这是一种集土地、水和碳排放于一体的解决方案。
Gensol Engineering战略咨询经理帕亚尔·萨克塞纳(Payal Saxena)表示，过去几年，太阳能市场的重点已从大型公用事业项目转向更具创新性地利用空间的较小项目，运河顶太阳能就是一个典型的例子。
2014年，古吉拉特邦750米的试点路段于2015年在古吉拉特邦瓦多达拉区建造了第一座大型运河顶部太阳能发电厂，耗资1830万美元。参与了第一个纳尔马达河运河项目的草根和农村创新发展公司首席执行官马尼克·乔利(Manik Jolly)表示，通往纳尔马达河的长运河使电力能够在多个地点提取。
乔利说，太阳能运河产生的能源可以在能源密集型灌溉季节为农民提供电力，而在反季节，电力可以送入国家电网，出售给配电公司或由运河管理局使用。由于这种电力是在农村地区生产的，因此降低了传输损耗，并加强了当地电网。
自从第一个太阳能运河项目以来，印度已经委托了其他一些项目，包括一个100兆瓦的运河顶部太阳能项目，位于纳尔马达河附近的支渠上，全长40公里，估计成本为10亿印度卢比(1390万美元/GB 930万)。
总体而言，古吉拉特邦有超过8万公里的运河蜿蜒流经该邦。根据古吉拉特邦电力公司的说法，如果其中30%转换为太阳能，可以产生18000兆瓦的电力，节省9万英亩的土地。
运河顶部的太阳能发电厂的优势不仅仅是当地的能源生产和节约土地。首先，太阳能发电站的建造速度可以比大型燃煤或天然气发电站快得多。另一方面，运河上的覆盖物有助于防止水分蒸发，为农作物和人类留下更多的可用资源。
总部位于艾哈迈达巴德的AHA太阳能光伏公司的创始董事Pulkit Dhingra指出，在古吉拉特邦、拉贾斯坦邦和马哈拉施特拉邦等运河主要用于灌溉的邦，蒸发是一个大问题。
太阳能板遮荫的另一个好处是抑制了运河中的藻类水华。藻类的生长会堵塞水泵并造成毒性。古吉拉特邦能源研究和管理研究所(Gujarat Energy Research And Management Institute)高级项目官员尼莱什·库马尔(Nilesh Kumar)表示：“由于安装了太阳能电池板，而且没有阳光直射，藻类的生长被极大地减少了。”
虽然水可以从上面的太阳能电池板中受益，但下面的太阳能电池板也是如此。自来水有助于电池板保持凉爽，从而使其效率至少提高2.5-5%。然而，也有一些缺点。运河顶部的发电厂比普通的太阳能发电厂建造成本更高。支架必须镀锌，因为下面的水会增加腐蚀的风险。丁格拉说，找到合适的位置也很困难，因为运河的宽度必须恰到好处。太宽了，建筑就会变得困难和昂贵。太窄了，可以建造的电池板太少，吸收不了足够的阳光。当然，必须有足够的运河长度来容纳计划中的太阳能结构。
而且，就像放置在陆地或屋顶上的太阳能电池板一样，如果上面聚集了灰尘，太阳能电池板必须定期清洗，因为发电量会下降。乔利说，运河顶部项目的运营和维护是一个巨大的挑战，因为必须修建坡道才能进行清洁。一些公司使用喷雾机和机器人来清洁偏远地区的面板。Gensol Engineering的Payal Saxena补充道：“由于运河顶部的植物分布在大片区域，无法通过边界墙或围栏进行保护，因此安全问题是主要的-可能必须安装摄像头来监控偷窃行为”，Gensol Engineering的佩亚尔·萨克塞纳(Payal Saxena)补充道。
运河蜿蜒的性质也带来了限制。为了最大限度地吸收能量，太阳能电池板应该朝南，但运河的方向不能被决定。太阳能电池板还会成为修复运河或清除淤泥的障碍，而且很多时候运河沿线的树木必须被砍伐，因为这些地区必须没有阴影。
到目前为止，印度已有8个邦委托进行运河太阳能项目。乔利说：“这些创新项目可以为数百万农民提供廉价和稳定的电力，并提高他们的利润。”环境保护基金(Environmental Defense Fund)分析师萨巴赫·乌斯马尼(Sabah Usmani)等研究人员表示，如果上方的太阳能发电场与下方的水力发电相结合，印度运河的可再生能源将有更大的潜力。
由于它们的水冷效率，以及对流经下面的运河的互惠互利，这些蜿蜒、闪闪发光的太阳能运河看起来很可能会成为印度更常见的景象。

note_en:
Solar energy is clean, but it usually takes up huge tracts of land. In India, an alternative is turning the country’s canals into glittering trails of solar panels.
 As the harsh midday sun beats down on a small, dusty village in Gujarat, western India, lines of blue solar panels on steel support structures snake their way to the horizon. The panels cover the top of irrigation canals, gleaming like iridescent mirrors. This small village of 40 homes with thatched walls and tin roofs, and lumbering stray cows, was one of rural India’s many communities who, until recently, did not have electricity. But now a lamp lights each home so children can study at night, and farmers can milk their cows long after sunset.
 India has relied traditionally on coal-fired power plants,  which generated 72% of the country’s electricity in 2018-19 . India’s combination of abundant sunshine – about 300 sunny days in a year – and a large energy-hungry population makes it  an ideal location for solar . The country’s  solar capacity reached 36.6GW at the end of the first quarter of 2020, with the aim of  growing to 100GW by 2022.
 But one of the main challenges in building solar farms is finding the right place to do it. Land is relatively expensive in India and often has multiple owners, so the purchase of land involves many formalities.  India’s high population density also puts pressure on the land, with an average of 464 people per square kilometre. Rooftop solar panels are one solution, but sunny space atop buildings is limited too.
 In Gujarat, the answer has been to cover its canals with solar panels, as a solution that saves land, water and carbon emissions in one.
   In the last few years, the solar energy market's focus has shifted from large-scale utility projects, to smaller projects that make more innovative use of space, says Payal Saxena, manager of strategy consulting at Gensol Engineering, and canal-top solar is a prime example.
  A pilot 750m stretch in Gujarat in 2014 led to the first large-scale  canal-top solar power plant in the Vadodara district of Gujarat in 2015, at a cost of $18.3 million. The long stretch of the canals leading off the Narmada River enables electricity to be extracted at multiple points, says Manik Jolly, chief executive of Grassroots and Rural Innovative Development, who was involved in the first Narmada River canal project.
 The energy generated from the solar canal can provide electricity for farmers during the energy-intensive irrigation season, and out of season the electricity can be fed into the state grid, sold to distribution companies or used by the canal authority, says Jolly. Since this power is generated in a rural area, the transmission losses are reduced and the local grid is strengthened.
 Since the first solar canal project, a number of others have been commissioned in India, including  a 100MW canal-top solar power project atop the branch canals off the Narmada River, stretching for a distance of 40km, at an estimated cost of 1bn Indian rupees ($13.9m/£9.3m).
 Overall, Gujarat has more than 80,000km of canals meandering through the state. According to Gujarat State Electricity Corporation,  if 30% of this were converted to solar, 18,000MW of power could be produced, saving 90,000 acres of land.
  The advantages of solar plants atop canals are not just about local energy production and land saved. For one thing, solar power plants can be built much faster than large coal or gas power stations. For another, the covering over the canal helps to prevent evaporation of water, leaving more available for crops and people.
  In states like  Gujarat, Rajasthan and Maharashtra, where canals are mainly used for irrigation, evaporation is a big problem, notes Pulkit Dhingra, founding director of Ahmedabad-based AHA Solar Photovoltaics.
 Another plus of the panels’ shade is curbing algal blooms in the canals. Algae growth can clog water pumps and cause toxicity. “With the installation of the solar panels, and absence of direct sunlight, algae growth is drastically minimised,” says Nilesh Kumar, senior project officer at the Gujarat Energy Research and Management Institute.
 And while the water can benefit from the solar panels above, so do the panels from the water below. The running water helps the panels to remain cool,  which increases their efficiency by at least 2.5-5%. There are some drawbacks, however. Canal-top plants are more expensive to construct than normal solar plants. The supports have to be galvanised with a protective zinc layer because the water below increases the risk of corrosion. Finding the right location is difficult too, as the width of the canal has to be just right, says Dhingra. Too wide, and the construction becomes difficult and expensive. Too narrow, and the number of panels that can be constructed are too few to absorb enough sunshine. And of course there has to be sufficient stretch of canal to accommodate the planned solar structure.
  And, like solar panels placed on land or rooftops, the panels must be cleaned regularly as electricity production declines if dust collects on top of them. The operation and maintenance of canal-top projects is a big challenge, as ramps have to be built to enable cleaning, says Jolly. Some companies  use sprayers and robots to clean the panels in remote areas. “Because canal-top plants are spread over large areas and cannot be protected by boundary walls or fencing, security concerns are major – cameras may have to be installed to monitor pilferage,” adds Gensol Engineering’s Payal Saxena.
 The meandering nature of the canals also imposes restrictions. To maximise the absorption of energy, the solar panels should face south, but the canal’s direction cannot be dictated.  Solar panels also act as an obstruction to repair the canals or remove silt, and very often trees along the canal have to be cut down as the areas have to be shadow-free.
 So far,  eight Indian states have commissioned canal solar projects. “These innovative projects can provide cheap and consistent electricity to millions of farmers and improve their profits,” says Jolly. And there is more potential to maximise renewable power from  India’s canals, if solar farms above were combined with hydro-power from below, as researchers such as Sabah Usmani, an analyst at the Environmental Defense Fund, have suggested.
 With their water-cooled efficiency, and mutual benefits for the canals that run beneath, it looks likely that these snaking, glittering solar canals will become a much more common sight in India.

title: 巧妙利用印度太空的“太阳能运河”
title_en: The ‘solar canals’ making smart use of India’s space

在上个月宣布PS5和Xbox Series X上NBA2K21的建议零售价高于正常水平70美元后，出版商Take-Two现在表示，提高价位可能不是其未来下一代游戏机游戏的标准。
首席执行官施特劳斯·泽尔尼克(Strauss Zelnick)在周一晚间的财报电话会议上表示，我们将明确宣布按标题定价。我只想观察一下，虽然成本大幅增加，但已经很长一段时间没有一线涨价了。
这是一个合理的观点：高端游戏的功能上限上一次上调是在2006年左右，当时随着Xbox360和PS3的推出，标准从50美元上涨到60美元。经通胀调整后，大预算游戏的最高要价从未像现在这样低，而开发成本也从未像现在这样高过。
尽管有这些压力，泽尔尼克还是不愿意把70美元定为他公司事实上的新标准定价。他说：我们重新采用这个价位，以防我们认为质量不仅支持它，而且要求它。生产成本大大提高了。但最重要的是，消费者体验比以往任何时候都更加强劲。我完全相信NBA2K21将会是非同寻常的，所以我不认为会有任何担忧。
Zelnick的评论是对该公司在7月份发表的一份不那么具体、更温和的声明的补充：2K对其游戏的建议零售价是为了代表所提供的价值，2K的一名代表当时表示。几乎无穷无尽的重放价值，以及许多新的添加和改进只能在下一代游戏机上实现，我们相信我们最新的建议零售价公平地代表了NBA 2K21的价值。&#34；
虽然它还处于游戏机过渡周期的早期阶段，但还没有其他发行商公开追随Take-Two进入70美元的游戏定价范围，即使是作为一个试验气球。相比之下，育碧首席执行官伊夫·吉利莫特在最近的一次财报电话会议上明确表示，2020年的圣诞游戏将以目前60美元的标准价格出售。
微软(Microsoft)的菲尔·斯宾塞(Phil Spencer)在最近接受“华盛顿邮报”(The Washington Post)采访时略微回避了70美元的游戏定价问题，没有对该公司未来自己的第一方游戏定价发表评论。
他告诉“华盛顿邮报”：“作为一个行业，我们可以为任何我们想要定价的东西定价，客户将决定对他们来说合适的价格是多少。”“我并不反对人们为游戏设定一个新的价格点，因为我知道每个人都会根据自己的业务需求做出自己的决定。但今天的游戏玩家拥有比以往更多的选择。归根结底，我知道客户可以控制他们支付的价格，我相信这个系统。“
尽管出版商对此保持沉默，但一些行业观察人士认为，在这一点上，新的、更高的价位几乎是不可避免的。
分析公司IDG的首席执行官大佐木义夫(Yoshio Osaki)上个月告诉GamesIndustry.biz，IDG与所有主要游戏发行商都有合作，我们的渠道调查显示，其他发行商也在探索提高某些特许经营权的下一代定价。虽然开发和出版的成本上升了，其他娱乐垂直市场的价格也大幅上升，但下一代软件的价格并没有反映出这些增长。59.99美元到69.99美元甚至不能完全覆盖其他成本的增加，但确实使其朝着正确的方向发展。
最终，这可能取决于游戏玩家用他们的钱包投票来决定每款游戏额外的10美元是否超过了某种消费临界点。“典型的游戏机玩家每年只购买几款完整的游戏，所以虽然10美元的涨价可能会减少利润率，但我也怀疑更多的游戏玩家会一直等到套装游戏或数字商店的价格降至60美元或50美元。”IDC分析师刘易斯·沃德(Lewis Ward)上个月告诉汤姆的指南。“因此，主要影响可能是将部分购买力有限的游戏玩家的销售推迟到较晚的日期。”
沃德继续说：“我们将拭目以待，看看这些游戏机上完整游戏的标准价格是不是(70美元)，但我的直觉告诉我，除非游戏玩家强烈反对，否则它很快就会成为常态。”
title: Take-Two表示，其70美元的游戏定价将以“逐个标题”为基础
title_en: Take-Two says its $70 game pricing will be on a “title by title basis”

note:
Google Play Music已经被谷歌判处死刑，今天该公司宣布了更多关于如何执行死刑的细节。今天这篇博文传达的主要信息是，现在就备份你的音乐，因为谷歌表示，它将在2020年12月清除所有谷歌音乐收藏。
我们早就知道停摆将在2020年的某个时候，但对于大多数地区，谷歌现在已经将停摆时间缩小到了10月份。以下是完整的时间表：
8月下旬-用户将不再能够通过音乐管理器上传或下载音乐。预购和购买将被关闭。
10月-全球流媒体关闭。谷歌音乐应用程序和网站将停止使用。
在流媒体关闭时，这款应用程序将已经显示关闭消息大约五个月了。如果一个用户不知何故错过了所有这些，两个月没有任何流媒体将有望足以让他们研究谷歌音乐发生了什么。
保存您的音乐收藏免于删除并不困难；只需转到www.music.youtube.com/transfer并单击几个按钮即可启动YouTube音乐传输流程。这个过程实际上是无痛的，即使在转移之后，你的谷歌音乐账号也会继续工作。如果你决定不想使用YouTube音乐，以后你仍然可以使用大量的下载选项，而不会面临谷歌音乐被删除的迫在眉睫的威胁。
你的文件副本也不难弄到。谷歌音乐管理器是一款Windows和Mac应用程序，只需点击几下就可以上传音乐或下载你的整个音乐收藏，但就像谷歌所说的那样，它很快就会停止工作。另一种选择是Google外卖，它会将你的整个音乐收藏打包成一个zip文件，并发送给你一个下载链接。这个过程可能需要几个小时。无论你选择哪个选项，一定要在12月之前做点什么，因为在那之后，你的音乐就没有办法恢复了。
Google Play Music自2011年以来一直存在，允许用户将数千首歌曲免费上传到互联网上，以便在大多数其他设备上进行流媒体播放。它一直是在谷歌家庭智能扬声器上播放音乐的主要方式，它还提供音乐购买、每月流媒体电台和播客。然而，谷歌音乐多年来一直被忽视，就像该公司经常做的那样，谷歌决定推出第二项与之竞争的音乐流媒体服务，而不是维持第一项服务。第二个服务是YouTube Music，它现在是谷歌最喜欢的音乐应用。这两家公司的合并最初是在2018年宣布的，现在终于实现了。
正如你从名字中可以猜到的那样，YouTube Music将重点放在音乐视频上，而且这款应用程序的设计更现代。YouTube Music笨拙地将你全部15年的YouTube活动历史与你的音乐收藏混合在一起，将任何被算法标记为音乐的喜欢的视频和订阅扔进你的收藏中，并将你的YouTube播放列表和谷歌音乐播放列表混合在一起。没有办法阻止这一切。
YouTube音乐似乎是为了提高谷歌的订阅量而设计的，而且似乎只对那些想要支付每月流媒体许可费的人有用。这款应用程序不再购买音乐，甚至不允许你在不支付月费的情况下将自己的音乐流式传输到你的谷歌家庭扬声器(Google Home扬声器)上。谷歌音乐为购买音乐的人提供了更多功能，这是谷歌音乐的一个大降级。
note_en:
Google Play Music has been given the death sentence by Google, and today the company  has announced a bit more detail about how its execution will be carried out. The main message from today's blog post is "back up your music now," as Google says it will wipe out all Google Music collections in December 2020.
 We've known  for a while that the shutdown would be sometime in 2020, but for most regions, Google has now narrowed it down to "October." Here's the full timeline:
 Late August—Users will no longer be able to upload or download music through Music Manager. Pre-orders and purchases will be shut down.
  October—Global streaming shutdown. The Google Music app and website will cease to be.
 At the time of the streaming shutdown, the app will have been showing shutdown messages for about five months. If a user has somehow missed all of those, two months with no streaming at all will hopefully be enough to get them to research what happened to Google Music.
 Saving your music collection from deletion is not difficult; just go to  music.youtube.com/transfer and click a few buttons to start the YouTube Music transfer process. The process  is actually painless, and your Google Music account will continue to work even after the transfer. If you decide you don't want to use YouTube Music, you'll still have access to a ton of download options later, without the looming threat of the Google Music deletion.
 A copy of your files isn't hard to get, either. The  Google Music Manager is a Windows and Mac application that can upload music or download your entire music collection with a few clicks, but like Google said, it will stop working soon. The other option is  Google Takeout, which will wrap your entire music collection in a zip file and send you a download link. The processing for this can take hours. Whichever option you choose, make sure you do something before December because, after that, there will be no way to recover your music.
 Google Play Music has been around  since 2011 and let users upload thousands of songs to the Internet, for free, for streaming playback on most other devices. It's been the primary way to play music on the Google Home smart speakers, and it offered music purchases, monthly streaming radio, and podcasts. Google Music has been neglected for years, though, and  like the company often does, Google decided to make a second, competing music streaming service instead of maintaining the first service. That second service is YouTube Music, which is now Google's favored music app. The merger between the two was  originally announced in 2018, and now it's finally happening.
 YouTube Music places an emphasis on music videos, as you can guess from the name, and the app has a more modern design. YouTube Music awkwardly blends together your entire 15-year YouTube activity history with your music collection, tossing any "liked" videos and subscriptions that have been algorithmically been flagged as "music" into your collection, and mixing together your YouTube playlists and Google Music playlists. There is no way to stop this.
 YouTube Music seems designed to drive up Google's subscription numbers and really only seems useful for people who want to pay the monthly streaming license fee. The app does away with music purchases and  won't even let you stream your own music to your Google Home speakers without paying the monthly fee. It's a big downgrade from Google Music, which offered more functionality to people who purchased music.
title: 谷歌音乐本月开始关闭，音乐于12月删除
title_en: Google Music shutdown starts this month, music deleted in December

今天，火狐推出了增强的跟踪保护(ETP)2.0，这是我们继续为用户提供安全和私密体验的下一步。ETP 2.0可保护您免受称为重定向跟踪(也称为退回跟踪)的高级跟踪技术的影响。我们将在接下来的几周内推出ETP 2.0。
去年，我们在Firefox中默认启用了ETP，因为我们认为了解广告跟踪行业的复杂性和成熟度不应该要求在线安全。ETP 1.0是我们在履行对用户的承诺方面迈出的重要的第一步。自从我们默认启用ETP以来，我们已经阻止了3.4万亿个跟踪cookie。在ETP 2.0中，Firefox为浏览器带来了更高级别的隐私保护。
自从引入ETP以来，广告业技术已经找到了跟踪用户的其他方法：创建变通方法和收集数据的新方法，以便在您浏览网页时识别您的身份。重定向跟踪绕过Firefox内置的第三方cookie拦截策略，在登陆您想要的网站之前通过跟踪器的网站。这使他们能够看到你从哪里来，你要去哪里。
在ETP 2.0中，Firefox用户现在将受到保护，不受这些方法的影响，因为它会检查是否需要每天删除来自这些跟踪器的cookie和站点数据。ETP 2.0阻止已知的跟踪器访问您的信息，即使是您可能无意中访问过的那些跟踪器。ETP 2.0每24小时从跟踪站点清除Cookie和站点数据。
有时追踪器不仅仅是追踪。他们还可能提供您参与的服务，例如搜索引擎或社交网络。如果火狐清除了这些服务的cookie，我们最终会让你每天从你的电子邮件或社交网络中注销，所以我们不会清除你在过去45天内与之互动的网站的cookie，即使他们是追踪者。这样，您就不会失去让您登录到您经常访问的站点的cookie的好处，也不会因为您访问过一次的站点而被无限期地跟踪。要阅读有关其工作原理的技术详细信息，请访问我们的安全博客文章。
这一切对你意味着什么？您只需使用Firefox继续浏览网页即可。我们正在采取更多措施自动保护您的隐私。无需更改设置或首选项，这种新的保护会删除使用变通方法跟踪您的cookie，这样您就可以高枕无忧了。
note_en:
Today, Firefox is introducing Enhanced Tracking Protection (ETP) 2.0, our next step in continuing to provide a safe and private experience for our users. ETP 2.0 protects you from an advanced tracking technique called   redirect tracking, also known as  bounce tracking. We will be rolling out ETP 2.0 over the next couple of weeks.
 Last year we  enabled ETP by default in Firefox because  we believe that understanding the complexities and sophistication of the ad tracking industry should not be required to be safe online. ETP 1.0 was our first major step in fulfilling that commitment to users. Since we enabled ETP by default, we’ve blocked 3.4 trillion tracking cookies. With ETP 2.0, Firefox brings an additional level of privacy protection to the browser.
 Since the introduction of ETP, ad industry technology has found other ways to track users: creating workarounds and new ways to collect your data in order to identify you as you browse the web. Redirect tracking goes around Firefox’s built-in third-party cookie-blocking policy by passing you through the tracker’s site  before landing on your desired website. This enables them to see where you came from and where you are going.
  With ETP 2.0, Firefox users will now be protected against these methods as it checks to see if cookies and site data from those trackers need to be deleted every day. ETP 2.0 stops known trackers from having access to your information, even those with which you may have inadvertently visited. ETP 2.0 clears cookies and site data from tracking sites every 24 hours.
 Sometimes trackers do more than just track. They may also offer services you engage with, such as a search engine or social network. If Firefox cleared cookies for these services we’d end up logging you out of your email or social network every day, so we don’t clear cookies from sites you have interacted with in the past 45 days, even if they are trackers. This way you don’t lose the benefits of the cookies that keep you logged in on sites you frequent, and you don’t open yourself up to being tracked indefinitely based on a site you’ve visited once. To read the technical details about how this works, visit our  Security Blog post.
 What does this all mean for you? You can simply continue to browse the web with Firefox. We are doing more to protect your privacy, automatically. Without needing to change a setting or preference, this new protection deletes cookies that use workarounds to track you so you can rest easy.
title: 最新的Firefox推出了增强的跟踪保护2.0；默认情况下会阻止重定向追踪器
title_en: Latest Firefox rolls out Enhanced Tracking Protection 2.0; blocking redirect trackers by default

积分计划是其他信用卡的“现金返还”服务的替代方案，后者立即奖励用户现金，他们可以保留或使用到下一张账单上。取而代之的是，Current的积分将在用户的账户下累积到一定的门槛，然后可以1美元兑100分的汇率兑换现金。它们还可以用于订阅。
目前，积分只适用于Current自己的会员订阅-该服务提供每月5美元的高级服务-但在未来，该公司设想使用积分来支付各种各样的订阅服务。
Current在发布时表示，大约50%的用户群生活在离报价2英里的范围内，但它正在努力将这一数字提高到100%。此外，即使在大流行期间，Current的用户也倾向于在他们所在的城市外出走动，因为大多数人(约80%)被归类为“基本工作者”。这包括那些在物流相关领域工作的人，如沃尔玛、亚马逊、Insta、DoorDash、Uber和Lyft，以及护士、军队和政府工作人员。
因为他们的工作，他们更有可能外出加油或在餐厅就餐，在那里他们可以很容易地获得积分。例如，目前估计，仅购买天然气一项，其成员每年就可以节省165美元的现金返还。
该公司表示，该公司是美国第一家推出积分奖励计划的新银行。尽管许多美国信用卡都提供积分或现金返还计划，但借记卡用户的庞大市场通常被忽视。(有几种基于奖励的借记卡，但为数不多)。
现任创始人兼首席执行官斯图尔特·索普(Stuart Sopp)解释说：“使用借记卡的人通常生活在一张又一张薪水上，而他们的钱要少得多。”他说，Current主要希望改善这些用户的财务状况，因为这是公司的核心使命。
然而，该计划也将让Current在越来越多的另类银行应用程序中脱颖而出，这些应用程序开始看起来都一样，这要归功于免费银行、免费现金取款和现代移动预算工具等消费者友好型功能的基线。
索普指出：“如果这意味着与Varo、Chime、Square和Venmo相比，在一个日益拥挤的市场中，用户认为我们更有价值，这是很有竞争力的。”他说：“我们非常自豪地伸出头来说，我们是第一家也是唯一一家提供积分的金融科技挑战者银行。”
随着时间的推移，积分计划还将有助于为Current创造额外的收入，因为它在银行和商家之间建立了一种关系-随着Current扩大其产品线，这一点可能会被证明是有价值的。
目前，Current正在利用几个未披露的第三方来帮助推动其积分计划，并结合内部努力-后者专注于让更大的品牌上岗。随着时间的推移，随着积分计划的发展，Current希望其商业合作伙伴将支付特权，让他们的优惠出现在最有可能兑换它们的用户面前。
索普说，这将不涉及共享用户的个人数据，而是将重点放在更合适地向最终用户提供优惠上。例如，一家餐厅的报价可能会出现在午餐时间左右。优惠也可以更精确的地理位置，在选择的基础上，所以当你走进商店时，你会得到一个关于优惠的提醒。
积分计划现在正在向Current的130万会员推出，既有基本的(免费的)，也有高级的(付费的)。目前的用户加在一起，到目前为止已经在手机银行存入了超过10亿美元。
note_en:
The points program is an alternative to other credit cards’ “cashback” offerings, which reward users immediately with cash they can keep or apply to their next bill. Instead, Current’s points will accumulate under a user’s account to certain thresholds, then can be redeemed for cash at a rate of 100 points per dollar. They can also be used for subscriptions.
 At the moment, the points can be applied only to Current’s own membership subscription — the service offers a Premium tier for $5 per month — but further down the road, the company envisions using points to pay for a wide variety of subscription services.
   At launch, Current says around 50% of its user base lives within 2 miles of an offer, but it’s working to get that number to 100%. In addition, Current’s users tend to be out and about in their city, even amid the pandemic, as the majority (around 80%) are classified as “essential workers.” This includes those who work in logistics-related fields, like Walmart, Amazon, Instacart, Doordash, Uber, and Lyft, as well as nurses, military, and government workers.
 Because of their jobs, they’re more likely to be out getting gas or eating at restaurants, where they could easily gain points. Current estimates its members will save $165 per year in cash back just from their gas purchases alone, for example.
 The company is the first neobank to roll out a points-based rewards program in the U.S., it says. Though plenty of U.S. credit cards offer points or cashback programs, the large market of debit card users is typically ignored. (There are  a few rewards-based debit cards, but they’re few and far between).
 “People who use debit typically live paycheck to paycheck and have far less money,” explains Current founder and CEO, Stuart Sopp. He says Current primarily wants to improve these users’ financial outcomes, as that’s the company’s core mission.
 However, the program will also allow Current to stand out among a growing number of alternative banking apps that are starting to all look the same thanks to a baseline of consumer-friendly features like no-fees banking, free cash withdrawals, and modern mobile budgeting tools, among other things.
 “If it means competitively — compared to Varo, Chime, Square and Venmo — that users in an increasingly crowded market see there’s more value with us, then that’s great,” notes Sopp. “We’re very proud to stick our head out and say, we’re the first and only  fintech   challenger bank to offer points,” he says.
  The points program, over time, will also help to generate additional revenue for Current as it establishes a relationship between the bank and merchants — something that could prove valuable as Current expands its product line-up.
 At the moment, Current is leveraging several undisclosed third-parties to help power its points program, combined with internal efforts — the latter focused on onboarding the larger brands. Over time, as the points program grows, Current hopes its merchant partners will pay for the privilege of having their offers surfaced to those users who are most likely to redeem them.
 Sopp says this wouldn’t involve sharing users’ personal data, but rather would focus on targeting offers more appropriately to end users. For instance, an offer for a restaurant may appear around lunch time. Offers could also be more precisely geolocated, on an opt in basis, so you’ll get an alert to an offer as you walk in a store.
 The points program is rolling out now to Current’s 1.3 million members, both Basic (free) and Premium (paid). Combined, Current users have deposited over $1 billion to date in the mobile bank.
title: 移动银行Current推出借记卡用户积分奖励计划
title_en: Mobile bank Current launches a points rewards program for debit card users

note:
这意味着这笔交易被搁置了一段时间，可能会持续到12月初。
欧盟委员会表示，它有90个工作日来就收购做出决定-因此到2020年12月9日。
欧盟委员会执行副总裁玛格丽特·维斯特格(Margrethe Vestager)负责欧盟的竞争政策和数字战略，她在一份声明中评论称：“未来几年，欧洲消费者对可穿戴设备的使用预计将大幅增长。”这将与通过这些设备产生的数据的指数增长齐头并进。这些数据提供了有关这些设备用户的生活和健康状况的关键洞察力。我们的调查旨在确保谷歌对交易导致的通过可穿戴设备收集的数据的控制不会扭曲竞争。“。
谷歌(Google)对欧盟遏制其雄心的回应是，其设备和服务主管在一篇博客文章中试图为这笔交易辩护，辩称这将刺激创新并导致竞争加剧。
谷歌副总裁里克·奥斯特罗进一步宣称：“这笔交易涉及的是设备，而不是数据。”
这家科技巨头早在去年11月就宣布希望进入Fitbit的数据集，当时它宣布计划以21亿美元全现金收购这家可穿戴设备制造商。
几个月后，首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)正因“帮助破坏互联网上的匿名性”等行为而受到国内立法者的指责。去年本已喧闹的反垄断鼓声在大型科技公司周围敲打，现在已经变成了一场充满激情的摇滚节，因此围绕科技公司收购的情绪音乐可能最终会发生变化。
自从谷歌(Google)计划收购Fitbit的消息传出后，整个欧洲都引发了对这笔交易的担忧-消费者团体、隐私监管机构以及竞争和技术政策专家都敲响了警钟，担心这家广告科技巨头可能会吞并一家设备制造商，并在此过程中帮助自己获取一系列敏感的消费者健康数据。
数字隐私权组织隐私国际(Privacy International)一直在敦促监管机构不要橡皮图章地批准这笔交易。该组织辩称，此次收购不仅会挤压新兴数字健康市场的竞争，也会挤压可穿戴设备的竞争，还会减少“谷歌目前在消费者(现有和未来的Fitbit用户)可用的隐私选择方面所面临的微不足道的竞争压力，导致隐私标准上的竞争变得更少，从而使消费者的隐私保护进一步恶化”。
噪音如此之大，以至于谷歌已经播放了“我们承诺不会…”的曲子。这张卡是数据挖掘技术巨头们的最爱。(通常在几年后，他们会说一句“我们抓到你了”的小丑--因为他们继续做他们完全不会做的事情。)。
也就是说，Fitbit从一开始就声称用户的“健康和健康数据不会用于谷歌广告”。就像WhatsApp说Facebook收购它们时什么都不会改变一样。(呃)。
上个月，路透社(Reuters)在一篇“独家”报道中重新讨论了这一让步，报道援引“知情人士”的话说，这些人显然告诉路透社，如果谷歌承诺不使用Fitbit数据投放广告，这笔交易可能会被否决。
目前尚不清楚其新闻报道的泄密来源，但路透社还引用了谷歌一位女发言人的话-她进一步声称：“在整个过程中，我们一直清楚地承诺，我们不会在谷歌广告中使用Fitbit健康数据，我们有责任为人们提供选择和控制他们的数据。”
最终，谷歌承诺用Fitbit数据表现自己，这一引人注目的承诺并未阻止欧盟监管机构介入更仔细地审视竞争问题--这恰恰是它应该做的。
事实上，鉴于目前人们对科技巨头的市场力量以及广告科技巨头谷歌(Google)专门攫取消费者健康数据宝库的担忧程度，全面调查是监管机构最起码应该做的事情。
如果说数字政策历史在过去十年+(以及数据方面)显示了什么，那就是魔鬼总是在细节中。此外，数字市场的快节奏可能意味着，竞争威胁可能只是一个微小的支点，距离实现只有一小步之遥。伤害理论显然需要更新，以考虑到数据挖掘技术社交平台巨头。欧盟委员会知道这一点-这就是为什么它正在咨询赋予自己更多权力，以解决数字市场中的小费问题。但它也需要灵活运用目前拥有的权力。比如展开适当的调查，而不是兴高采烈地挥舞着科技巨头的交易。
反垄断可能是本月科技巨头们关注的焦点-在上个月国会小组委员会的大对决中，美国立法者几乎向数字“强盗大亨”宣战。但也值得注意的是，欧盟竞争监管机构-尽管他们关于适当监管数字领域的言论大肆宣传-但尚未阻止一起数字技术合并。
欧盟委员会在今天的一份新闻稿中写道：“欧盟委员会担心，拟议中的交易将增加谷歌本已可以用来对其服务和显示的广告进行个性化处理的海量数据，从而进一步巩固谷歌在在线广告市场的市场地位。”
在对该交易进行初步评估后，欧盟监管机构表示，他们对(强调他们的)感到担忧：
交易对在线搜索和展示广告服务供应的影响(分别在互联网搜索引擎的结果页或其他互联网页面上出售广告空间)。
以及“提供‘广告技术’服务(用于促进数字广告的程序化销售和购买的分析和数字工具)”。
“通过收购Fitbit，谷歌将获得(I)Fitbit维护的有关其用户健康状况的数据库；以及(Ii)开发与Fitbit类似的数据库的技术，”欧盟委员会进一步指出。
“在欧盟委员会审查交易的这个阶段，通过手腕佩戴的可穿戴设备收集的数据似乎是在线广告市场的一项重要优势。通过增加谷歌在通过其搜索引擎提供的广告个性化方面的数据优势，并在其他网页上显示广告，竞争对手将更难与谷歌的在线广告服务相匹敌。因此，这笔交易将提高谷歌竞争对手获得这些服务的进入和扩张门槛，最终损害广告商和出版商的利益，因为它们将面临更高的价格，选择更少。“。
欧盟委员会认为，谷歌在几乎所有欧洲经济区(EEA)国家的在线搜索广告服务供应方面占据主导地位；在大量EEA国家(特别是非社交网络展示广告)的在线广告展示服务供应方面拥有“强大的市场地位”，在EEA的广告技术服务供应方面拥有“强大的市场地位”。
当谷歌考虑是否会通过吸收Fitbit数据获得不公平的竞争优势时，所有这些都将成为其考虑的依据。(维斯特格近年来还针对这家科技巨头发布了一系列反垄断执法措施，针对安卓(Android)、AdSense和谷歌购物(Google Shopping)。)。
“Fitbit和谷歌的数据库和功能在数字医疗领域的结合所产生的影响，该领域在欧洲仍处于初级阶段”
“一旦谷歌拥有Fitbit，它是否有能力和动机降低竞争对手的可穿戴设备与谷歌Android智能手机操作系统的互操作性？”
这家科技巨头已经向欧盟监管机构提供了一个具体的让步，希望让Fitbit Buy变得绿灯-欧盟委员会指出，它上个月提交了旨在解决担忧的承诺。
谷歌建议建立一个数据竖井，以保存通过Fitbit可穿戴设备收集的数据，并表示这些数据将与谷歌内部的任何其他数据集分开保存(包括声称出于广告目的将受到限制)。然而，欧盟委员会对谷歌的提议表示怀疑，写道，它“认为谷歌提出的数据竖井承诺不足以明确打消现阶段对交易影响的严重疑虑”。
“除了其他原因，这是因为数据竖井补救措施没有涵盖谷歌将因这笔交易而访问的所有数据，对于广告目的将是有价值的，”该公司补充道。
谷歌在其博客文章中提到了这个数据竖井，声称：“我们从一开始就很清楚，我们不会在谷歌广告中使用Fitbit的健康和健康数据。我们最近提出就我们对Fitbit数据的使用向欧盟委员会做出具有法律约束力的承诺。正如我们对所有产品所做的那样，我们将让Fitbit用户选择查看、移动或删除他们的数据。我们将继续支持我们和其他公司产品的广泛连通性和互操作性。“。
“我们很感激有机会与欧盟委员会合作，制定一种方法来满足消费者对其可穿戴设备的期望。我们相信，通过与Fitbit的专家团队密切合作，并结合我们在人工智能、软件和硬件方面的经验，我们可以为世界各地的人们制造令人信服的设备。“
note_en:
This means the deal is on ice for a period of time that could last until early December.
 The Commission said it has 90 working days to take a decision on the acquisition — so until December 9, 2020.
  Commenting on opening an “in-depth investigation” in a statement, Commission EVP Margrethe Vestager — who heads up both competition policy and digital strategy for the bloc — said: “The use of wearable devices by European consumers is expected to grow significantly in the coming years. This will go hand in hand with an exponential growth of data generated through these devices. This data provides key insights about the life and the health situation of the users of these devices.Our investigation aims to ensure that control by Google over data collected through wearable devices as a result of the transaction does not distort competition.”
 Google has responded to the EU brake on its ambitions with a  blog post in which its devices & services chief seeks to defend the deal, arguing it will spur innovation and lead to increased competition.
 “This deal is about devices, not data,” Google VP Rick Osterloh further claims.
 The tech giant announced its desire to slip into Fitbit’s data-sets back in  November, when it announced a plan to shell out $2.1BN in an all-cash deal to pick up the wearable maker.
 Fast forward a few months and CEO  Sundar Pichai   is being taken to task by lawmakers on home turf for stuff like ‘ helping destroy anonymity on the Internet‘. Last year’s already rowdy antitrust drum beat around big tech has become a full on rock festival so the mood music around tech acquisitions might finally be shifting.
 Since news of Google’s plan to grab Fitbit dropped concerns about the deal have been raised all over Europe — with  consumer groups,  privacy regulators and  competition and tech policy wonks all sounding the alarm at the prospect of letting the adtech giant gobble a device maker and help itself to a bunch of sensitive consumer health data in the process.
 Digital privacy rights group,  Privacy International — one of the not-for-profits that’s been urging regulators not to rubberstamp the deal — argues the acquisition would not only squeeze competition in the nascent digital health market, and also for wearables, but also reduce “what little pressure there currently is on Google to compete in relation to privacy options available to consumers (both existing and future Fitbit users), leading to even less competition on privacy standards and thereby enabling the further degradation of consumers’ privacy protections”, as it puts it.
 So much noise is being made that Google has already played the ‘we promise not to…’ card that’s a favorite of data-mining tech giants. (Typically followed,  a few years later, with a ‘we got ya sucker’ joker —  as they go ahead and do the thing they totally said they wouldn’t.)
 To wit: From the get-go Fitbit has  claimed users’ “health and wellness data will not be used for Google ads”. Just like WhatsApp said nothing would change when Facebook bought them. ( Er.)
 Last month Reuters revisited the concession, in an “exclusive” report that cited “people familiar with the matter” who apparently told it the deal could be waved through if Google pledged not to use Fitbit data for ads.
 It’s not clear where the leak underpinning its news report came from but Reuters also ran with a quote from a Google spokeswoman — who further claimed: “Throughout this process we have been clear about our commitment not to use Fitbit health and wellness data for Google ads and our responsibility to provide people with choice and control with their data.”
 In the event, Google’s headline-grabbing promises to behave itself with Fitbit data have not prevented EU regulators from wading in for a closer look at competition concerns — which is exactly as it should be.
 In truth, given the level of concern now being raised about tech giants’ market power and adtech giant Google specifically grabbing a treasure trove of consumer health data, a comprehensive probe is the very least regulators should be doing.
 If digital policy history has shown anything over the past decade+ (and where data is concerned) it’s that the devil is always in the fine print detail. Moreover the fast pace of digital markets can mean a competitive threat may only be a micro pivot away from materializing. Theories of harm clearly need updating to take account of data-mining technosocial platform giants. And the Commission knows that — which is why it’s  consulting on giving itself more powers to tackling tipping in digital markets. But it also needs to flex and exercise the powers it currently has. Such as opening a proper investigation — rather than gaily waving tech giant deals through.
 Antitrust may now be flavor of the month where tech giants are concerned — with US lawmakers all but declaring war on digital ‘robber barons’ at last month’s  big subcommittee showdown in Congress. But it’s also worth noting that EU competition regulators — for all their heavily publicized talk of properly regulating the digital sphere — have yet to block a single digital tech merger.
  “The Commission is concerned that the proposed transaction would further entrench Google’s market position in the online advertising markets by increasing the already vast amount of data that Google could use for personalisation of the ads it serves and displays,” it writes in a  press release today.
 Following a preliminary assessment process of the deal, EU regulators said they have concerns about [emphasis theirs]:
 “the impact of the transaction on the supply of  online search and display advertising services (the sale of advertising space on, respectively, the result page of an internet search engine or other internet pages)”
 and on “the supply of  ‘ad tech’ services (analytics and digital tools used to facilitate the programmatic sale and purchase of digital advertising)”
 “By acquiring Fitbit, Google would acquire (i) the database maintained by Fitbit about its users’ health and fitness; and (ii) the technology to develop a database similar to Fitbit’s one,” the Commission further notes.
 “The data collected via wrist-worn wearable devices appears, at this stage of the Commission’s review of the transaction, to be an important advantage in the online advertising markets. By increasing the  data advantage of Google in the personalisation of the ads it serves via its search engine and displays on other internet pages, it would be more difficult for rivals to match Google’s online advertising services. Thus, the transaction would raise barriers to entry and expansion for Google’s competitors for these services, to the ultimate detriment of advertisers and publishers that would face higher prices and have less choice.”
 The Commission views Google as dominant in the supply of online search advertising services in almost all EEA (European Economic Area) countries; as well as holding “a strong market position” in the supply of online advertising display services in a large number of EEA countries (especially off-social network display ads), and “a strong market position” in the supply of adtech services in the EEA.
 All of which will inform its considerations as it looks at whether Google will gain an unfair competitive advantage by assimilating Fitbit data. (Vestager has also issued a number of antitrust enforcements against the tech giant in recent years, against  Android,  AdSense and  Google Shopping.)
  the “effects of the combination of Fitbit’s and Google’s databases and capabilities in the  digital healthcare sector, which is still at a nascent stage in Europe”
 “whether Google would have the ability and incentive to degrade the  interoperability of rivals’ wearables with Google’s Android operating system for smartphones once it owns Fitbit”
 The tech giant has already offered EU regulators one specific concession in the hopes of getting the Fitbit buy green lit — with the Commission noting that it submitted commitments aimed at addressing concerns last month.
 Google suggested creating a data silo to hold data collected via Fitbit’s wearable devices — and where it said it would be kept separate from any other dataset within Google (including claiming it would be restricted for ad purposes). However the Commission expresses scepticism about Google’s offer, writing that it “considers that the data silo commitment proposed by Google is insufficient to clearly dismiss the serious doubts identified at this stage as to the effects of the transaction”.
 “Among others, this is because the data silo remedy did not cover all the data that Google would access as a result of the transaction and would be valuable for advertising purposes,” it added.
 Google makes reference to this data silo in its blog post, claiming: “We’ve been  clear from the beginning that we will not use Fitbit health and wellness data for Google ads. We recently offered to make a legally binding commitment to the European Commission regarding our use of Fitbit data. As we do with all our products, we will give Fitbit users the choice to review, move or delete their data. And we’ll continue to support wide connectivity and interoperability across our and other companies’ products.”
 “We appreciate the opportunity to work with the  European Commission   on an approach that addresses consumers’ expectations of their wearable devices. We’re confident that by working closely with Fitbit’s team of experts, and bringing together our experience in AI, software and hardware, we can build compelling devices for people around the world,” it adds.
title: 谷歌-Fitbit交易将因数据竞争担忧在欧洲接受审查
title_en: Google-Fitbit deal to be scrutinized in Europe over data competition concerns

吉姆·法利(Jim Farley)已被任命为总裁兼首席执行官，许多人认为他是在培养这一职位。法利于2007年加入福特，担任全球营销和销售主管。他在公司的队伍中一直走到2019年，当时他被选中领导福特的新业务、技术和战略团队。他在2月份被任命为首席运营官，这一决定与福特汽车总裁乔·辛里奇的突然退休一起，似乎决定了法利成为首席执行官的道路。
领导层的更迭和哈克特的离职将于10月1日生效，哈克特将继续担任福特的特别顾问，直至2021年3月。
哈克特说：“当我担任首席执行官时，我的目标是为福特未来的胜利做好准备。”对于一家自豪的、长寿的公司来说，最难做的事情是改变，以迎接它正在进入的世界的挑战，而不是它已经知道的世界。我为我们在创造现代福特汽车方面取得的进展感到非常自豪，我对未来非常乐观。“。
哈克特的扭亏为盈计划旨在使公司现代化，同时让公司变得更健康，这是他在被任命为首席执行官几个月后于2017年使用的一个术语。该计划包括在五年内削减140亿美元的成本，福特表示，这一目标可以通过在整个投资组合中增加通用部件的使用，减少原型制造，以及减少客户可以订购的可能选项组合的数量来实现。
福特还计划将重点转向其赚钱企业-也就是提供最高毛利率的卡车和SUV。三年前，该公司表示，将从汽车领域撤出70亿美元，并将其投入到这些利润中心，如福特F-150、游骑兵(Ranger)和全新的野马(Bronco)。自那以后，福特宣布计划停止在北美销售汽车-野马除外-转向卡车和SUV。这家汽车制造商已经停止生产福特金牛座(Ford Taurus)、嘉年华(Fiesta)和C Max。
它还包括一项指令，要求向电动汽车投入更多资金，将连通性作为优先事项，并通过增加机器人、3D打印和虚拟现实工具来实现福特工厂的现代化，以加快其汽车的设计、开发和生产。
哈克特实现了这些目标中的许多，但在其他方面做得不够，特别是在制造和运输车辆的日常辛劳方面。尽管推出了全电动野马Mach-E，重新设计的福特F-150和新的Bronco系列，福特的股价仍然萎靡不振。该公司因新冠肺炎疫情而陷入瘫痪，这打乱了其精简海外业务的计划，并导致其在第二季度关闭了北美的工厂。
与此同时，由于车辆存在一些质量问题，该公司的保修成本也出现了飙升。并不是每一辆车都发射得很顺利。例如，重新设计的福特探索者(Ford Explorer)的推出出现了失误，使公司倒退。
尽管如此，福特执行主席比尔·福特(Bill Ford)在周二的一份声明中称赞了哈克特的领导能力。
福特说：“我非常感谢吉姆·哈克特(Jim Hackett)为福特的现代化所做的一切，并让我们为未来的竞争和胜利做好准备。”“我们的新产品愿景--由野马Mach-E、新型F-150和野马家族引领--正在形成。我们现在对电动汽车和自动驾驶汽车以及全车辆连接都有令人信服的计划。我们正变得更加灵活，这一点在大流行开始时我们迅速动员起来制造救生设备时就很明显了。“
note_en:
Jim Farley,   who many believed was being groomed for the position, has been named president and CEO. Farley joined Ford in 2007 as global head of marketing and sales. He moved through the ranks of the company until 2019 when he was picked to lead Ford’s new businesses, technology and strategy team. He was named COO in February, a decision that along with the sudden retirement of Ford’s president of automotive, Joe Hinrichs, seemed to seal Farley’s path to CEO.
 The change to leadership and Hackett’s exit will become effective October 1. Hackett will continue as a special advisor to Ford through March 2021.
  “My goal when I took on the CEO role was to prepare Ford to win in the future,” Hackett said. “The hardest thing for a proud, long-lived company to do is change to meet the challenges of the world it’s entering rather than the world it has known. I’m very proud of how far we have come in creating a modern Ford and I am very optimistic about the future.”
 Hackett’s turnaround plan was aimed at  modernizing the company while making it “fitter,” a term he used back in 2017 several months after being named CEO. That plan included $14 billion in cost reductions over five years, a target that Ford said it could reach by increasing the use of common parts across its portfolio, building fewer prototypes and reducing the number of possible option combinations customers could order.
 Ford also planned to shift its focus to its money makers — aka the trucks and SUVs that provided the heftiest gross margins. Three years ago, the company said it would take $7 billion away from cars and put it to work in those profit centers, vehicles like the Ford F-150, Ranger and the all-new Bronco. Ford has since announced plans to  stop selling cars in North America — with the exception of the Mustang — in its shift to trucks and SUVs. The automaker has already stopped producing the Ford Taurus, Fiesta and C Max.
 It also included a directive to put more money into electric vehicles, make connectivity a priority and modernize Ford’s factories by adding robotics, 3D printing and virtual reality tools to speed up the design, development, and production of its vehicles.
 Hackett accomplished many of those goals, but fell short in others, particularly around the day-to-day toil of making and shipping vehicles. Despite the launch of the all-electric Mustang Mach-E, a redesigned Ford F-150 and new Bronco series, Ford’s share price languished. The company has been hamstrung by the COVID-19 pandemic, which has disrupted its plans to streamline operations overseas and caused it to shut down factories in North America in the second quarter.
 Meanwhile, the company has seen warranty costs balloon due to some quality problems with its vehicles. Not every vehicle launch went smoothly either. For instance, the rollout of the redesigned Ford Explorer was blundered and set the company back.
 Still, Ford Executive Chairman Bill Ford, in a statement Tuesday, credited Hackett for his leadership.
 “I am very grateful to Jim Hackett for all he has done to modernize Ford and prepare us to compete and win in the future,” said Ford. “Our new product vision — led by the Mustang Mach-E, new F-150 and Bronco family — is taking shape. We now have compelling plans for electric and autonomous vehicles, as well as full vehicle connectivity. And we are becoming much more nimble, which was apparent when we quickly mobilized to make life-saving equipment at the outset of the pandemic.”
title: 福特任命吉姆·法利为新任首席执行官，吉姆·哈克特退休
title_en: Ford names Jim Farley as new CEO, Jim Hackett to retire

苹果宣布更新27英寸和21.5英寸的iMac。它正在将内部处理器升级到英特尔的第十代彗星湖处理器，并将网络摄像头切换到更高分辨率的1080p高清传感器，而不是其他Mac电脑中的低分辨率720p。固态硬盘现在是全行业的标准配置，将会有一系列新的配置选项-包括反射率较低的哑光玻璃的“纳米纹理”版本。
27英寸的iMac起价1799美元，今天就可以订购，将于本周发货。21.5英寸的iMac起价1099美元，将于下周发货。苹果还对iMac Pro进行了小幅规格提升，使10核英特尔芯片成为新的基线，售价4999美元-也将于下周发货。(奇怪的是，iMac Pro在其他方面没有更新。)。
这不是传言(和希望)的重大重新设计，也不是第一款基于ARM的Mac。取而代之的是，2020款iMac看起来与上一代一模一样，包括屏幕周围的黑色大表圈和底部的大金属下巴。苹果公司表示，正如它在WWDC上指出的那样，它打算在未来很多年里支持基于英特尔的Mac电脑。
对于大多数用户来说，网络摄像头可能是最大的日常升级。它是1080p，但苹果公司表示，新的T2芯片除了提高分辨率外，还提供了一些新功能，如色调映射、曝光控制和面部检测。苹果公司表示，改进后的摄像头功能将适用于任何视频会议应用程序，而不仅仅是FaceTime。
不过，不要对面部检测感到太兴奋。这只是为了确保你的脸在视频通话中被正确暴露。它无法解锁iMac。事实上，尽管内置了新的T2芯片，但新的iMac没有任何生物识别登录选项；没有面部识别，也没有指纹传感器。苹果表示，许多客户使用Apple Watch自动登录。
不过，T2芯片确实支持其他一些新功能，包括支持“嘿Siri”，这是一种新的音频控制器，应该会在较低的音量下改善音频低音和保真度，以及显示器的True Tone。请注意，扬声器和显示器的实际硬件没有变化。更新来自T2控制器。不过，麦克风升级后采用了三麦克风“录音棚品质”阵列。
尽管这不是一个大的重新设计，但粉丝最喜欢的选择可能是新的纳米纹理涂层，它最终为iMac带来了一个哑光选项。然而，它给价格增加了500美元的溢价。苹果公司表示，对于这笔钱，iMac得到了与Pro Display XDR相同的待遇。所以你得到了完全相同的好处(最小的雾霾)和缺点(需要一块特殊的布来清洁)。
至于核心规格，英特尔的6核和8核版本将是标配，10核i9涡轮增压高达5.0 GHz是可供选择的。标准显卡配置有AMD的RADEON 5300或5500XT，带有16 GB VRAM的5700XT可作为升级。这些iMac还可以拥有高达128 GB的DDR4RAM。
在标准配置中切换到固态硬盘而不是Fusion驱动器早就应该了-它们在基本配置中将是256 GB。但是，如果用户想要更多存储而不需要花钱购买更大的固态硬盘，则可以免费选择1 TB Fusion Drive。如果用户确实想花更多的钱购买更大的固态硬盘，如果他们愿意，他们可以一直到8TB。苹果表示，SSD应支持高达3.4Gbps的读/写速度。
还有其他各种配置选项和升级：10千兆位以太网是一个升级选项，SD卡读卡器(幸好仍然存在)可以支持UHS-II速度。
IMac的规格提升似乎不足以吸引拥有上一代iMac的用户升级。但是，如果你一直在等待一体机系统的更新，它们可能值得一看。我们会尽快测试它的速度。
note_en:
Apple is announcing updates for both the 27-inch and 21.5-inch iMacs. It’s updating the processors inside to Intel’s 10th Gen Comet Lake processors and also switching out the webcam to a higher-resolution 1080p HD sensor instead of the low-res 720p found in other Macs. SSDs are now standard across the line, and there will be a bunch of new configuration options — including a “nano-texture” version with less reflective matte glass.
 The 27-inch iMac starts at $1,799, is available to order today, and will ship this week. The 21.5-inch iMac starts at $1,099 and will ship next week. Apple is also giving the iMac Pro a small spec bump, making the 10-core Intel chips the new baseline for $4,999 — also shipping next week. (Oddly, the iMac Pro is not otherwise getting updated.)
 This is not the major redesign that has been rumored (and hoped for), nor is it the first ARM-based Mac. Instead, the 2020 iMacs look identical to the last generation, including the large black bezel all the way around the screen and the big metal chin at the bottom. Apple says that, as it noted at WWDC, it intends to support Intel-based Macs for many years to come.
   The webcam might be the biggest day-to-day upgrade for most users. It’s 1080p, but Apple says that the new T2 chip enables some new features beyond the improved resolution like tone mapping, exposure control, and face detection. Apple says the improved camera features will work in any videoconferencing app, not just FaceTime.
  Don’t get too excited about that face detection, though. It’s just for ensuring your face is properly exposed in video calls. It can’t unlock the iMac. In fact, despite the new T2 chip inside, the new iMac doesn’t have any biometric login options; there’s no Face ID nor is there a fingerprint sensor. Apple says many of its customers use the Apple Watch to automatically log in.
 The T2 chip does allow for some other new features, though, including support for “Hey Siri,” a new audio controller that should improve audio bass and fidelity at lower volumes, and True Tone for the display. Note that the actual hardware for the speakers and display is unchanged. The updates come from the T2 controller. The microphones  are updated, though, with a three-mic “studio quality” array.
 Even though it’s not a big redesign, the fan-favorite option might be the new nano-texture coating, which finally brings a matte option back to the iMac. It adds a $500 premium to the price, however. For that money, Apple says the iMac receives the same treatment as on the  Pro Display XDR. So you get the exact same benefits (minimal haze) and downsides ( requiring a special cloth to clean it).
  As far as core specs go, Intel’s 6- and 8-core versions will be standard, with a 10-core i9 that turbo boosts up to 5.0GHz available as an option. Standard graphics configs have AMD’s Radeon 5300 or 5500XT, with the 5700XT with 16GB VRAM available as an upgrade. These iMacs can have up to 128GB of DDR4 RAM as well.
 The switch to SSDs instead of Fusion Drives in the standard configs is long overdue — they’ll be 256GB in the base configs. However, if users want more storage without ponying up for bigger SSDs, a 1TB Fusion Drive will be available as a no-cost option. If users  do want to spend more for a bigger SSD, they can go all the way up to 8TB if they’d like. Apple says the SSDs should support up to 3.4Gbps read/write speeds
 There are various other config options and upgrades: 10-gigabit Ethernet is an upgrade option, and the SD card reader (blessedly still present) can support UHS-II speeds.
 The spec bumps for the iMac don’t seem like quite enough to entice users who have the last-generation iMac to upgrade. But if you’ve been waiting for an update for the all-in-one system, they might be worth a look. We’ll be putting one through its paces as soon as we can.
title: 苹果用新的英特尔处理器和更好的网络摄像头更新了iMac
title_en: Apple Updates the iMac With New Intel Processors and a Better Webcam

今年的末日是星期二，我邀请了一位朋友来参加一场双人对决：一轮Stet！，这是根据“Dreyer‘s English”改编的新语言游戏；接着是马克·艾伦(Mark Allen)的“That Word Chat”节目，这是一档为墨水部落的编辑、词典编纂者、语言学家和其他人制作的自制Zoom脱口秀节目。我的朋友是梅里尔·珀尔曼(Merrill Perlman)，她为“哥伦比亚新闻评论”(Columbia News Review)撰写“语言角”专栏，在她的简历中写道，她“管理过纽约时报编辑部的文案写字台”。虽然已经从全职新闻业退休，但她继续任教，并在编辑协会的董事会任职。她的手机整个下午都在断断续续地嘟嘟声，每个新闻机构都有独特的音调。“那是”泰晤士报“，”她会说，“那是”华盛顿邮报“，或者”那是CNN。“。她从一开始就击败了我。
吹毛求疵的人，我们马上就遇到了问题。给斯特特的指示！建议你“与三个或更多的玩家一起玩”(这是多余的吗？)，在大流行期间，我们一直无法吓到第三个书呆子。斯泰特的游戏！包括两包写有句子的卡片，其中50张是有无可争辩的错误的语法卡片(悬垂的修饰语，臭气熏天的撇号，以及同音异义词，如部落/囤积和统治/缰绳)，50张是风格卡片，上面的句子是正确的，但平淡无奇，目的是在不重写句子的情况下改进句子。有没有错误的魔术牌。你可能会怀疑(剧透提醒)“杰克逊·波洛克”、“阿萨菲达”或“农贸市场”有什么问题，但这些都是转移视线。如果你认为句子是完美的，你可以喊“Stet！”，这是“别管它”的校对术语(源自拉丁语，意思是“让它原地踏步”)，文案编辑用它来保护作者的散文，作者用它来保护他们的散文不受文案编辑的影响。
这个游戏包括一些角色扮演。如果你只使用语法卡，发牌者被称为抄写长，就像“抄写长洗五十张语法卡”中所说的那样。如果你把样式牌混在一起，发行人就是作者，玩家就是文案编辑(你几乎可以听到一个作者喃喃地说，“每个人都是文案编辑”)，而且这副牌很大。我从卡片的大小中得到的印象是，作者和文案编辑的手很大，很有阳刚之气。这些卡片比塔罗牌里的卡片都要大。我个人戴着一只中小型的一次性腈纶手套，不能用任何形式的才华来装饰甲板(或者是“照明弹”？)。
斯泰特身上的运动元素！当你发现一个或多个错误时，正在用手拍打经过仔细消毒的桌子。分数是根据句子中错误的数量而定的。大多数人只有一个，有些人有两个，还有几个三分球。对遗漏的错误进行处罚，但不对引入错误的处罚，这是复制编辑中的大罪。(也许说明书可以改进一下，为这件事多加一记耳光。)。赢得一场比赛需要5分，而且比赛进行得很快。我轻而易举地赢了第一轮，主要是因为我的对手，抄写长，总是忘了打耳光。
这款游戏是本杰明·德雷尔(Benjamin Dreyer)于2019年出版的“德雷尔英语”(Dreyer‘s English)的衍生游戏，兰登书屋的作者是兰登书屋的文案主管。出现了一些模式。德雷尔坚决反对裁员，比如“她耸耸肩”和“她点头”(她还会耸耸肩或点头吗？)。和空的强化词(非常，真的，实际上)，他对所谓的“僵尸规则”不屑一顾，比如那些反对分裂不定式和以介词结束句子的规则。在玩游戏的时候，我们自己的动作很快就变得明显起来：我一直反对使用分号；美林想要颠倒属性的顺序，这样它就会显示为“莫蒂默说”，而不是听起来更浮夸的“莫蒂默说”。我们在鸡尾酒时间停止计分，在甲板上疾驰而过，与成为我们荣誉第三名的德雷尔斗智斗勇，因为他的印记在这场比赛上到处都是。我们的判决是：斯特特！是准备文案编辑测试的绝佳方式，与一杯不错的杜松子酒和奎宁水搭配得很好。
巴士司机的假期继续着新一集的“那个词聊天”(每周二4：30)。。E.D.T.)。我们坐在桌子的两端，我坐在笔记本电脑上，梅里尔坐在她的平板电脑上，走进我们的Zoom包厢，恭敬地将自己静音。主持人马克·艾伦(Mark Allen，Twitter上的@EditorMark)是董事会的一名独立编辑，他在今年春天酝酿了这个节目的想法，以期在封锁期间让呆在家里的文案编辑们振作起来。他从俄亥俄州贝克斯利的家庭办公室出发，穿着轻便的西装，打着蝴蝶结，看起来衣冠楚楚，背景是一架架的时尚指南。他的嘉宾包括许多一线语言专家，其中包括大卫·克里斯特尔、罗伊·彼得·克拉克、杰西·谢德尔、林恩·墨菲、彼得·索科洛夫斯基、科里·斯坦珀、安蒙·谢伊、简·弗里德曼和米格农·福加蒂(又名Mignon Fogarty)。语法女孩。质量
今年夏天早些时候，大卫·雷姆尼克(David Remnick)给“纽约客”(The New York Ker)的员工写了一封信，表示出于尊重，该杂志今后将利用布莱克，加入“泰晤士报”、“华尔街日报”和美联社(The Associated Press)的行列。我们给人们起他们想被叫的名字，如果他们改变了他们想被叫的名字，就像有时发生的那样，我们会再次改变。在做出这一决定时，雷姆尼克是从W·E·E·B·杜·布伊斯(W.E.E.B.Du Bu Bois)1899年的一项研究中推断出来的，在那项研究中，作者在脚注中写道，他将把黑人这个词从头到尾都大写，“因为我相信800万美国人有权使用大写字母。”
当世界末日接近尾声时，我们关掉了我们的设备，把我们的游戏放在一边，我意识到Stet！卡片虽然对性别敏感，但还没有涉及种族问题。毫无疑问，出版商正在计划出版第二版。
note_en:
Nerdsday fell on a Tuesday this year, and I invited a friend over for a doubleheader: a round of Stet!, the new language game based on “Dreyer’s English,” followed by an episode of Mark Allen’s “That Word Chat,” a homespun Zoom talk show for editors, lexicographers, linguists, and others of the inky tribe. My friend was Merrill Perlman, who writes the column “Language Corner” for the  Columbia Journalism Review, where her biographical note says that she has “managed copy desks across the newsroom at the  New York  Times.” Although retired from full-time journalism, she continues to teach and serves on the board of  : The Society for Editing. Her cell phone beeped intermittently all afternoon, a unique tone for each news outlet. “That’s the  Times,” she’d say, or “That’s the Washington  Post,” or “That’s CNN.” She outnerded me from the start.
  Nitpickers by profession, we ran into a problem right away. The instructions for Stet! suggest that you “play with three or more players” (is that redundant?), and we had been unable, during  the pandemic, to scare up a third nerd. The game of Stet! comprises two packs of cards with sentences on them, fifty of them Grammar cards with indisputable errors (dangling modifiers, stinking apostrophes, and homonyms, like horde/hoard and reign/rein) and fifty of them Style cards, on which the sentences are correct but pedestrian, and the object is to improve the sentence without rewriting it. There are trick cards with no mistakes on them. You might suspect that there is something wrong with (spoiler alert) “Jackson Pollock” or “asafetida” or “farmers market,” but these are red herrings. If you believe that the sentence is perfect just as it is, you shout “Stet!,” the proofreading term for “leave it alone” (from the Latin for “let it stand”), which is used by copy editors to protect an author’s prose and by authors to protect their prose from copy editors.
 The game involves some role playing. If you use only the Grammar cards, the dealer is called the Copy Chief, as in “The Copy Chief shuffles the fifty Grammar cards.” If you mix in the Style cards, the dealer is the Author, the players are Copy Editors (you can almost hear an author muttering, “Everyone is a copy editor”), and the deck is huge. I got the impression from the size of the cards, which are bigger than those in a tarot pack, that authors and copy editors have large, masculine hands. I personally wear a small-to-medium-sized disposable nitrile glove and could not riffle the deck with any kind of flair (or is it “flare”?).
  The sporting element in Stet! is slapping your hand on the carefully sanitized table when you spot the mistake or mistakes. Points are awarded based on the number of errors planted in a sentence. Most have just one, some have two, and there are a few three-pointers. Penalties are assessed for missing mistakes, but none for introducing an error, a cardinal sin in copy editing. (Perhaps the instructions could be refined to add a slap on the hand for this.) It takes five points to win a game, and the game goes fast. I won the first round handily, mostly because my opponent, the Copy Chief, kept forgetting to slap.
 The game is a spinoff of “ Dreyer’s English,” by Benjamin Dreyer,  published in 2019 by Random House, where the author is the copy chief. A few patterns emerged. Dreyer is firmly against redundancies such as “she shrugged her shoulders” and “she nodded her head” (what else would she shrug or nod?) and empty intensifiers (very, really, actually), and he dismisses so-called “zombie rules,” like those against splitting infinitives and ending a sentence with a preposition. While playing, our own tics quickly became evident: I kept objecting to semicolons; Merrill wanted to reverse the order of an attribution so that it read “Mortimer said” instead of the more pompous-sounding “said Mortimer.” We stopped keeping score around cocktail time and zipped through the deck, matching wits with Dreyer, who became our honorary third, as his stamp is all over this game. Our verdict: Stet! is an excellent way to prepare for a copy-editing test and pairs well with a nice gin-and-tonic.
  The busman’s holiday continued with a new episode of “That Word Chat” (Tuesdays at 4:30  . . E.D.T.). We settled in at opposite ends of the table, I on my laptop and Merrill on her tablet, entered our Zoom boxes, and reverently muted ourselves. The host, Mark Allen (@EditorMark on Twitter), an independent editor who is on the board of  , hatched the idea for the show this past spring as a way to cheer up housebound copy editors during the lockdown. He Zooms from his home office, in Bexley, Ohio, looking dapper in a lightweight suit and bow tie, with shelves of style guides in the background. His guests have included many A-list language pundits, among them David Crystal, Roy Peter Clark, Jesse Sheidlower, Lynne Murphy, Peter Sokolowski, Kory Stamper, Ammon Shea, Jane Friedman, and Mignon Fogarty, a.k.a. Grammar Girl. Mark’s son, James Allen, a composer who lives in Cincinnati, wrote  theme music for the show, and, though it took a few weeks for Mark to master the technology and cue up the music (it helped when Heather Saunders joined the show as a producer), it has proved to be quite catchy, with strong earworm potential. Several episodes of the show have been edited and posted on YouTube. (Full disclosure: I appeared in the pilot, which was full of glitches and will probably never see the light of day. Also, the puttanesca sauce I demonstrated in my home kitchen was pasta poison.)
 Mark’s guests that day were the linguists James Harbeck (@sesquiotic), Zooming from downtown Toronto, and Stan Carey (@StanCarey), who lives near Galway, in Ireland. Together they created  Strong Language, “a sweary blog about swearing.” It went up in December of 2014 and has had no shortage of contributors or material. The two reminisced fondly about “the ‘shithole’ quote that Trump gave us.” Journalists and translators all over the world had to figure out how to translate “shithole” after Trump was quoted, on the front page of the  Times, complaining about immigrants from “shithole countries.” In China, it came out as the place “where birds don’t lay eggs.” Countries with strong religious traditions have a rich trove of ecclesiastical terms (Jesus H. Christ!  Porca Madonna!) to combine with the usual excretory and sexual profanities. In Quebecois,  tabarnak (tabernacle) is highly offensive. Carey was asked about the word “shite.” I always thought it was a euphemism for “shit,” but it turns out to be a centuries-old British and Irish variant of “shit” with a vowel sound that can be extended for greater satisfaction. “Shit’s very versatile,” Harbeck said sagely. In Scottish English, “cunt” is a pronoun (“If anycunt is accepted . . .”). By the end, the chat window was a bloody mess, with members of the audience contributing their favorites: shitgibbon, twat weasel, cockwobble, bawbag . . . This was profanology at its fucking finest.
  Between news flashes, Merrill fielded an e-mail from an editor at the  Columbia Journalism Review about one of this season’s most heated language wars—the status of “irregardless”—though for Merrill reheated would be more accurate. She had  written about “irregardless” back in 2008, when purists were shocked to find the word in the dictionary, although it had been there for decades. Here it was again, and she would  tackle the subject anew. (Basically, if people use it, it’s a word, and it goes in the dictionary. If you don’t like it, you don’t have to use it, but before you complain about “irregardless” being accepted by Webster’s Dictionary of English Usage (gasp!), note that the citation is from a story by Ring Lardner, who used substandard English for comic effect.) Merrill  wrote about the capitalization of Black in 2015, after the mass shooting of Black people by a white supremacist in Emanuel African Methodist Episcopal Church, in Charleston. The usage did not catch on at the time, but it has been in the air, and once we start capitalizing a word, we get used to it. With the urgency of the Black Lives Matter movement, the  Columbia Journalism Review added an editor’s note to the column, updating its usage.
 Earlier this summer,  David Remnick wrote a letter to the staff of  The New Yorker saying that the magazine would henceforth capitalize Black, as a matter of respect, joining the  Times, the  Wall Street Journal, and the Associated Press. We call people what they want to be called, and if they change what they want to be called, as sometimes happens, we change it again. In reaching this decision, Remnick had extrapolated from an 1899 study by W. E. B. Du Bois, in which the author wrote in a footnote that he would capitalize the word Negro throughout, “because I believe that eight million Americans are entitled to a capital letter.”
 As Nerdsday drew to a close and we turned off our devices and put away our game, I realized that the Stet! cards, though sensitive to gender, did not address race—yet. No doubt the publishers are planning a second edition.

note:
特朗普总统周一两次表示，作为监管部门批准的条件，美国财政部需要获得TikTok销售价格的一部分。
重要原因：这类似于敲诈勒索--这是你会期望在窃听中听到的事情，而不是在记者面前从白宫听到的。
特朗普声称，他对禁止TikTok的威胁，以及由此引发的激烈的并购程序，都是基于国家安全方面的考虑。
但是，这与要求支付交易审批费用是不一致的，即使这样的事情是合法的。一份销售协议要么满足国家安全标准，要么不符合。
言外之意：这是因为正如特朗普也说的那样，微软并不是唯一一家踢TikTok轮胎的公司。多位消息人士告诉Axios，苹果已经表达了兴趣，尽管苹果内部没有消息来源，而且至少还有一家战略公司表达了兴趣。
考虑到TikTok是一个跨平台的应用程序，而且比蒂姆·库克(Tim Cook)可能想要的(无论是在这里还是在中国)更令人头疼的政治问题，这对苹果来说将是一笔不同寻常的交易。但该公司手头肯定有现金。
苹果公司的一位发言人告诉Axios，目前还没有关于收购TikTok的讨论，公司也不感兴趣。
大局：在定价方面，TikTok目前的所有者ByteDance显然理应有多个追求者。多个追求者也可以帮助白宫获得让步。
私募股权也在徘徊，包括一些目前没有ByteDance股份的公司。但没有消息来源指出，有一家公司非常乐观地认为，他们可以在没有找到内部人士或重大战略合作伙伴的情况下完成这一任务。
倒叙：这让人想起华为是如何在美中贸易谈判中被用作筹码的，似乎它的危害性随着中国同意购买的大豆数量的增加而时好时坏。
在总统发表上述言论之前不久，白宫高级贸易顾问彼得·纳瓦罗(Peter Navarro)在接受“Axios Re：Cap”采访时表示，买家可能不得不做出不同的让步，比如减少在中国的非TikTok商业活动。
现状：ByteDance不可能将部分销售收益支付给美国财政部，即使只是因为在中国的公关影响。
即使字节跳动允许TikTok的买家在其销售协议中编入这样一笔款项(这是值得怀疑的)，它(几乎)也无法想象有任何美国公司会这样做。这是一个行动公园级别的滑坡道。
一句话：不能保证特朗普的9月1日会达成协议。15号截止日期，更不用说他声称要求的条款了。
如果不能，他将不得不在选举前不到两个月禁止一项受到数千万选民喜爱的服务，否则他将不得不拖延时间。
note_en:
President Trump said twice Monday that the U.S. Treasury would need to get a portion of the sale price of TikTok, as a condition of regulatory approval.
 Why it matters: This is akin to extortion — the sort of thing you'd expect to hear on a wiretap, not from the White House in front of reporters.
 Trump has claimed that his threats to ban TikTok, and the furious M&A process it spawned, are based on national security concerns.
 But, this is inconsistent with requiring payment for deal approval, even if such a thing were legal. Either a sale agreement satisfies national security criteria or it doesn't.
 Between the lines: That's because Microsoft isn't the only party kicking TikTok's tires, as Trump also said. Multiple sources tell Axios that Apple has expressed interest, albeit no sources inside of Apple, and that at least one other strategic has expressed interest.
 It would be an unusual deal for Apple, given that TikTok is a cross-platform app, and a bigger political headache than Tim Cook may want (both here and in China). But the company certainly has the cash on hand.
 An Apple spokesperson told Axios there are no discussions about buying TikTok, and the company isn’t interested.
 The big picture: It obviously behooves current TikTok owner ByteDance for there to be multiple suitors when it comes to pricing. Multiple suitors also could help the White House secure concessions.
 Private equity is also circling, including some firms that don't have current stakes in ByteDance. But no sources have noted one that's terribly optimistic that they can pull it off without finding an insider or major strategic with whom to partner.
 Flashback: It's reminiscent of how Huawei was used as a chip in U.S.-China trade negotiations, as if its perniciousness waxed and waned with the number of soybeans China agreed to buy.
 The president's comments came shortly after an  "Axios Re:Cap" interview with Peter Navarro, in which the White House's top trade adviser suggested that a buyer may have to offer different concessions, such as reducing non-TikTok business activities in China.
 The state of play: There is no way ByteDance would pay a portion of sale proceeds to U.S. Treasury, if only because of the PR ramifications in China.
 Even if ByteDance would allow TikTok's buyer to codify such a payment in its sale agreement, which is doubtful, it's (almost) inconceivable that any U.S. corporation would do so. It's an Action Park-level slippery slope.
 The bottom line: There is no guarantee a deal will be struck by Trump's Sept. 15 deadline, let alone one on the terms he claims to require.
 If not, he'll either have to ban a service beloved by tens of millions of voters, less than two months before the election, or kick the can down the road.
title: 苹果表示，目前还没有就收购TikTok进行谈判，也不感兴趣，此前有消息称，苹果是表示有兴趣收购TikTok的公司之一
title_en: Apple says there are no talks to buy TikTok, and it isn't interested, after sources said it is among the companies that have expressed interest in buying TikTok

我们提供了来自哈萨克斯坦的家猫(Felis catus L.，1758)的最早证据，它是一具保存完好的骨骼，具有广泛的骨学病理，可以追溯到公元775-940年，来自哈萨克斯坦中世纪早期的城市占肯特。这个城市定居点位于北部丝绸之路的交汇处，这条丝绸之路将南部的霍雷兹市与北部伏尔加地区的贸易定居点连接起来，在公元10世纪被称为游牧民族奥古兹的首府。这种家猫的存在，在这里呈现为结合了动物考古学、遗传学和同位素数据的骨生物学，为以前牧区内人与动物关系性质的根本转变提供了代理证据。这说明了在中世纪早期丝绸之路沿线快速城市化的背景下发生的更广泛的社会、文化和经济变化。
家猫和野猫的埋葬在考古记录中仍然是罕见的，特别是与狗相比，狗被发现的频率如此之高，以至于它们有自己的沉积类型1、2、3、4、5。在考古背景下发现个别的、有关节的动物，特别是那些表现出骨病理学的动物，是社会动物考古学方法的理想选择，这种方法鼓励将人与动物的互动作为超出生产力的社会和文化结构的一个方面进行调查6。考古学中这种类型的综合社会文化方法在很大程度上得到了应用。然而，在历史背景下利用这一框架是有价值的，因为一些动物，如家猫，在书面记录中往往不经常和明确地被提及。这些动物是改变文化态度的标志，也是人类介导的物种传播的证据，伴随着人类连通性的扩大，直到公元7，8世纪末。在哈萨克斯坦中世纪市中心占肯特发现的一种独特而罕见的小型猫科动物遗骸，适合这种方法的时机已经成熟。下面，我们结合多条科学调查路线：探索这只猫的祖先，确定它的家养状况；与其他动物相比，描述它的饮食特征；并重建它的生活史。因此，这一骨生物记录反映了人类对动物态度的变化，这是中世纪丝绸之路上发生的更大的社会和文化变化的一部分。
在中亚半干旱干旱的草原上，特别是在今天的哈萨克斯坦，在十八世纪和十九世纪殖民时期之前，家猫并不普遍。哈萨克大草原的任何史前考古遗址9、10、11、12都没有已公布的家猫考古遗迹，尽管越来越多的证据表明，该地区从青铜时代晚期开始的定居和农业活动将鼓励猫13、14、15等共生啮齿动物猎人的存在和利用。沿着中亚草原的南部边界，在乌兹别克斯坦和土库曼斯坦，在青铜发现了驯养状态不确定的猫科动物的单一骨骼。以及来自Geoktchick Depe和Kyzyltepe的铁器时代背景(图1)16，17。小型猫科动物的遗骸在该地区的整个古代和古董时期(公元前4世纪到公元前8世纪)同样稀少，在城市遗址如Kugaitepe，Tukatepe，Tok-Kala，Toprak-Kala，Koi Krilgan Kala，Kuruk Kala和蛇发女怪中只发现了一块骨骼。在今天的俄罗斯，在公元1000年之前的现代俄罗斯北部也有类似稀少的猫科动物遗骸，在此之后，猫的遗骸在莫斯科以北的诺夫哥罗德和特维尔等地点更常见，更接近喀山和托雷茨克草原边界21、22、23。
文本中提到的占肯特和其他考古遗址的位置。标有广场的地点是当时的城市中心。在qgis(www.qgis.org)和Adobe Illustrator(www.adobe.com)中由作者生成的地图；底图数据OpenStreet(www.openstreetmap.org)，根据CC by-SA2.0许可进行改编(https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/)，边界矢量文件dcw(https://worldmap.harvard.edu/data/geonode:Digital_Chart_of_the_World?fbclid=IwAR2aFoqB1oWgay4fFXp0IifLZvsJobf0zaMYhYJo1H7TD5JHrX-RmaAA_1g).。
猫科动物在这个地区的考古学背景下最常被发现，不是作为骨骼遗骸，而是作为艺术装饰品和特征。

骨骼元素呈现阴影；所有恢复的元素都记录了古病理。左插图：左肱骨关节骨折的闭合。右插图：右股骨三维模型的X线片和视图。
通过骨骺融合和牙齿萌发，这只猫死亡时至少58周大；然而，整个骨骼中与年龄相关的骨病的存在表明，这只猫可能比下限估计的37，37，38岁更老。将四肢骨骼的最大长度数据与已公布的俄罗斯考古背景下的同期家猫、带有性别鉴定数据的现代英国家猫和野猫以及已公布的欧洲家猫和野猫的标准进行比较，结果表明，这种动物的大小与完整(未阉割)的雄性家猫相似，但比欧洲野猫小(补充表2)23、39、40。对形态特征的宏观检查，以及与莱斯特大学考古和古史学院收藏的现代比较动物考古收藏品的比较，以及地区特有的比较文学，对于将该标本鉴定为物种的广泛病理学掩盖了定性鉴别特征41，42没有定论。
最近的遗传学研究表明，所有现代家猫种群都起源于非洲野猫，即猫科动物猫科动物7、8、25、43、44。然而，解开家猫和野猫的遗传结构是具有挑战性的，因为在8，43，45个谱系之间正在进行杂交。据报道，中亚野猫(F.L.ornata)具有高频率的非洲野猫线粒体单倍型(高达43只采样猫的一半)，这使得线粒体DNA不适合于祖先鉴定。与线粒体DNA相比，核数据揭示了更清晰的种群结构，野猫和家猫形成了截然不同的核分支8，43。因此，我们利用核DNA的遗传分析来探索占肯特标本的祖先。
从岩骨中提取DNA，并使用专门的古代DNA协议46、47、48将其转换为单链测序文库。产生了大约52个百万次读取，其中48%未能通过最小长度过滤器(&lt； 30个bp)。在剩下的片段中，79%的片段可以定位到家猫(Felis Catus)的参考基因组49，这表明该标本的岩骨含有很高的内源性含量。这与之前对人类50块岩骨和洞穴熊51块岩骨的研究结果一致。常染色体的平均覆盖率约为0.38-0.43x，X染色体组装的平均覆盖率约为0.22 × ：X染色体的覆盖率降低是一个强烈的迹象，表明占肯特人是男性。Dzankent猫的线粒体单倍型属于一个包含家猫(F·L·lybica)单倍型的分支(补充图。1)，但这提供了微弱的祖先证据。为了进一步研究这一点，我们基于低覆盖率的Dzhankent猫的核基因组数据和代表多个猫种的公开数据集进行了群体聚类分析。这一分析表明分类群8，43有明显的分离(图4a；补充表3)，其中最大的成分代表了过滤数据中超过35%的变异。Dzhankent猫标本与家猫成簇(图4a)。对野生欧洲野猫(F.silvestris)和家猫(F.catus)的第二次分析显示了相同的聚类(图4b)。对整个核基因组的系统发育分析支持包括Dzhankent猫在内的家猫的单系性，它们共同形成了欧洲野猫基因组的姐妹群(图4c)。
多物种基因组数据的主成分分析(A)和家猫和野猫基因组数据(B；补充表3)，以及家猫、Dzankent猫和一只野猫核基因组的相邻连接系统发育图(C)。分析基于928,665(A)、764,913(B)和727,798(C)过滤后的可变位置(见方法)。A和B中的轴标签表示每个分量解释的差异百分比。邻接的系统发育以黑脚猫(F.nigripes)为外类群(未示出)。颜色反映了传说中所示的(亚)物种。
不幸的是，在这个项目进行的时候，中亚野猫F.L.ornata还没有核猎枪基因组数据，这使得现代的中亚野猫数据和古代的Dzankent猫无法进行直接比较。以前对从现代猫科动物中收集的核数据(短串联重复序列和微卫星43)的研究确实表明，中亚野猫在基因上与d野猫是不同的。
碳和氮稳定同位素在欧亚大陆52、53、54、55被常规用于重建人类和动物的饮食。关于家养动物的饮食摄入量也有越来越多的证据，特别是那些可能作为同伴(如狗和猫)或吃了人类粪便56、57、58的动物。为了澄清研究中的猫的饮食摄入量，我们分析了来自占肯特地区的动物的稳定碳(δ13C)和氮(δ15N)同位素(n = 34)，并将我们的结果与之前公布的更广泛地区的同位素值进行了比较(图5)。
